title: Sonatype 2026 State of Software Supply Chain Reports 1.23 Million Cumulative Malicious Packages with 454600 New in 2025
description: Sonatype's 2026 State of the Software Supply Chain Report counted more than 1,233,000 malicious open source packages cumulatively across npm, PyPI, Maven Central, NuGet, and Hugging Face, with 454,600 newly identified in calendar 2025 alone representing a 75 percent year-over-year jump, with the open source ecosystem reaching 9.8 trillion package downloads in 2025.
label: AV-200

title: Verizon 2025 DBIR Documents Third-Party Supply Chain Share of Breaches Doubling from 15 to 30 Percent in Single Year
description: Verizon's 2025 Data Breach Investigations Report documented the third-party supply chain share of breaches doubling from 15 to 30 percent in a single annual reporting cycle, representing the largest single-year shift in the DBIR series history and confirming software supply chain attacks as the most reliable attack pattern entering 2026.
label: AV-509

title: IBM 2025 Cost of Data Breach Report Prices Supply Chain Compromise at 4.91 Million Dollars Average with 267-Day Lifecycle
description: IBM's 2025 Cost of a Data Breach Report priced supply chain compromise at a US$4.91 million average cost with a 267-day mean lifecycle from breach to containment, making supply chain attacks the longest and most expensive breach vector of any category analyzed in the annual report.
label: AV-509

title: Repository Abuse Found in 55.9 Percent of All Logged Malicious Packages as Attackers Treat npm and PyPI as Marketing Automation Platforms
description: Stingrai Research documented that repository abuse appeared in 55.9 percent of all logged malicious packages, with attackers treating npm and PyPI like marketing automation platforms by publishing iteratively, swapping email aliases, and repeating the cycle to maximize malicious package distribution while evading registry security measures.
label: AV-200

title: ENISA Threat Landscape 2025 Attributes 10.6 Percent of Security Incidents to Software Supply Chain Attacks Across 4875-Incident Corpus
description: The ENISA Threat Landscape 2025 report attributed 10.6 percent of all analyzed security incidents to traditional software supply chain attacks across a 4,875-incident corpus, establishing supply chain attacks as a statistically significant and growing category of cyber threat requiring dedicated defensive strategies.
label: AV-509

title: May 11 2026 TanStack UiPath OpenSearch and Guardrails AI Attacked in Largest Coordinated Registry Poisoning Spanning Both npm and PyPI
description: On May 11 2026, a coordinated supply chain attack compromised over 170 npm packages and 2 PyPI packages totaling 404 malicious versions, hitting the TanStack router ecosystem, Mistral AI SDK on both npm and PyPI, UiPath automation tooling across 65 packages, OpenSearch with 1.3 million weekly npm downloads, and Guardrails AI in the first documented campaign spanning both npm and PyPI in a single coordinated operation.
label: AV-410

title: UiPath 65 npm Packages Compromised in TanStack Wave 4 Attack with Bug Rendering Payload Non-Functional in UiPath and Mistral Packages
description: Further analysis of the May 11 2026 TanStack supply chain attack revealed that while 65 UiPath automation tooling npm packages and Mistral AI SDK packages were compromised, a bug in the payload rendered the malware non-functional specifically in the UiPath and Mistral packages, limiting the actual credential theft impact to the @tanstack namespace.
label: AV-410

title: Shai-Hulud Here We Go Again Variant Infects 170 Packages with 180 Million Weekly Downloads Creating 350 GitHub Dead Drop Repositories
description: A Shai-Hulud variant dubbed Here We Go Again began infecting over 170 different npm packages collectively counting nearly 180 million weekly downloads, with infected packages uploading encrypted stolen credentials to victim GitHub accounts under new repositories with names drawn from the Dune universe, creating over 350 such dead drop repositories within 24 hours.
label: AV-410

title: TeamPCP Open Sources Mini Shai-Hulud Malware on May 13 2026 Enabling Any Attacker to Deploy Automated Supply Chain Worms
description: Hackers behind the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attacks released an open-source version of their malware on May 13 2026, paving the way for more automated worms carrying infections downstream and enabling any attacker regardless of technical sophistication to deploy supply chain worm attacks using the proven toolchain.
label: AV-410

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Now Includes Wiper Module Enabling Data Destruction as Fallback When Credential Exfiltration Fails
description: The latest Mini Shai-Hulud variant documented in May 2026 included a wiper module that attempted to destroy data on compromised systems when credential exfiltration to primary ICP canister and GitHub dead drop channels failed, adding destructive capability to the credential theft and self-propagation functions of the supply chain worm.
label: AV-400

title: NHS England Digital Issues CC-4781 Cyber Alert Warning All NHS Organizations of TanStack Mistral AI UiPath and OpenSearch Compromise
description: NHS England Digital issued cyber alert CC-4781 warning all NHS organizations of the May 2026 supply chain attack affecting TanStack, Mistral AI, UiPath, and OpenSearch packages, with malicious packages uploaded in two phases on April 29 and May 11 2026 containing heavily obfuscated payloads designed to execute during installation or import.
label: AV-410

title: Compromised Publishing Credential Grants Unrestricted Access to Publish New Versions Identified as Root Cause Across 2026 Supply Chain Campaigns
description: SafeDep security researchers identified compromised publishing credentials granting unrestricted access to publish new package versions as the consistent root cause across all major 2026 supply chain attack campaigns, from the single-package axios compromise to the multi-namespace TanStack campaign targeting hundreds of packages simultaneously.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Next.js next.config.js Alter Security Headers and Content Security Policy Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Next.js configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter security header definitions and Content Security Policy directives, causing Next.js applications to serve pages with different security headers from what developers intend when reviewing application security configuration.
label: AV-300

title: XZ Utils CVE-2024-3094 Jia Tan Backdoor Hooks RSA_public_decrypt via GNU IFUNC Gated on Specific Ed448 Key for SSH Authentication Bypass
description: The XZ Utils backdoor CVE-2024-3094 injected obfuscated object code into liblzma via a malicious M4 macro in the build system, hooking RSA_public_decrypt through GNU IFUNC and gating remote code execution on a specific Ed448 key, with the payload activating through the systemd-sshd dependency chain affecting major Linux distributions.
label: AV-400

title: Jia Tan XZ Utils Social Engineering Exploited Original Maintainer Lasse Collin Feeling Overwhelmed and Seeking Help
description: The XZ Utils backdoor social engineering campaign exploited original maintainer Lasse Collin publicly posting about feeling overwhelmed and looking for help maintaining the project, with Jia Tan stepping up as a helpful contributor before slowly gaining trust and ultimately maintainer access enabling the two-year supply chain attack.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Nx workspace.json and project.json Alter Build Target Commands and Executor Configurations
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Nx monorepo workspace.json and project.json configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter build target executor commands and configuration parameters, causing Nx build pipelines to execute different build and deploy commands from what engineers review during monorepo configuration audits.
label: AV-300

title: Package Manager Guard PMG Dependency Cooldown Policy Blocks Newly Released Packages from Immediate Installation to Prevent Fast-Moving Attacks
description: SafeDep's Package Manager Guard tool implements a dependency cooldown policy that blocks newly released package versions from being installed immediately after publication, reducing developer exposure to fast-moving supply chain attacks like the May 2026 TanStack compromise by creating a mandatory waiting period for security vendor detection.
label: AV-509

title: AI and ML Packages Mistral AI SDK and Guardrails AI Targeted Alongside Web Frameworks Suggesting Attackers Target Broadest Possible Developer Population
description: The inclusion of AI and ML packages including Mistral AI SDK on both npm and PyPI and Guardrails AI alongside web framework packages like TanStack and enterprise automation tooling like UiPath in the May 2026 attack suggested attackers deliberately targeted the broadest possible developer population rather than any specific technology vertical.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Storybook Configuration Files Alter Component Story Loading and Addon Configuration
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Storybook configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter story loading patterns and addon configurations, causing Storybook to load stories from different locations and execute different addon behaviors from what frontend developers intend during component documentation configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: OpenSearch npm Package with 1.3 Million Weekly Downloads Compromised as Collateral Victim in May 2026 TanStack Wave 4 Attack
description: The OpenSearch npm package with 1.3 million weekly downloads was compromised as a collateral victim in the May 2026 TanStack Wave 4 supply chain attack, demonstrating that supply chain worm self-propagation mechanisms affected packages outside the primary target namespace creating unintended collateral impact across the broader npm ecosystem.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in SvelteKit svelte.config.js Alter Adapter Configuration and Build Output Targets
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in SvelteKit svelte.config.js configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter adapter configuration and build output target settings, causing SvelteKit applications to be deployed to different hosting environments or with different build configurations from what developers intend during configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Dependency Layer No Longer a Side Channel It Is the Channel as Supply Chain Attacks Dominate 2026 Threat Landscape
description: Stingrai Research concluded that the software dependency layer is no longer a side channel for attackers but has become the primary attack channel, with three forces driving the 2026 numbers: industrialization of malicious package publication, 9.8 trillion package downloads providing massive attack surface, and registries unable to deplatform malicious packages faster than attackers publish them.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Playwright and Cypress Test Configuration Files Alter Browser and Test Environment Settings
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Playwright and Cypress end-to-end test configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter browser selection, base URL configurations, and test environment settings, causing integration tests to run against different environments or with different browser configurations from what QA teams review in test setup files.
label: AV-300

title: guardrails-ai PyPI Package Compromised Alongside Mistral AI in Cross-Ecosystem npm and PyPI Supply Chain Attack Wave
description: The guardrails-ai Python package on PyPI was compromised alongside the Mistral AI SDK in the May 2026 cross-ecosystem supply chain attack wave, marking one of the first documented cases where a single coordinated supply chain attack simultaneously targeted both the npm JavaScript and PyPI Python package ecosystems within the same campaign.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in tailwind.config.js Alter Class Generation Rules and Theme Security Token Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Tailwind CSS configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter safelist patterns and theme configuration values, causing Tailwind to generate different CSS utility classes and expose different design token values from what developers intend during CSS framework configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Two-Phase Attack April 29 and May 11 2026 Shows TeamPCP Uses Staged Publication to Test Detection Before Full Campaign Launch
description: NHS England Digital documented that the TanStack supply chain attack used two publication phases on April 29 and May 11 2026, suggesting TeamPCP deliberately staged the malicious package publication to test detection capabilities and registry response times before launching the full-scale coordinated campaign targeting all 42 TanStack packages simultaneously.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Nuxt.js nuxt.config.ts Alter Server Middleware and Route Rule Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Nuxt.js configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter server middleware registration order and route rule definitions, causing Nuxt applications to apply different middleware and routing logic from what developers review when configuring server-side rendering and API route security.
label: AV-300

title: Ox Security Confirms More Than 350 GitHub Dune-Named Dead Drop Repositories Created by Shai-Hulud Here We Go Again Worm
description: Ox Security confirmed that the Shai-Hulud Here We Go Again variant created more than 350 GitHub repositories with names drawn from the Dune science fiction universe as dead drops for stolen encrypted credentials, using victim GitHub account tokens to create and populate the repositories with credential data accessible to attackers via the GitHub API.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Prisma Schema Generate Different TypeScript Client Types from Database Model Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Prisma database schema definitions where hidden Unicode characters cause the Prisma client generator to produce TypeScript types with different field definitions and relation types from the schema's visual representation, potentially allowing type-unsafe database access patterns to bypass TypeScript compile-time checks.
label: AV-300

title: Mistral AI SDK Compromised on Both npm and PyPI Simultaneously Showing Supply Chain Worm Cross-Registry Propagation Capability
description: The Mistral AI SDK was compromised on both npm and PyPI simultaneously in the May 2026 TanStack wave, demonstrating that supply chain worms have achieved cross-registry propagation capability enabling a single campaign to spread malicious package versions across multiple package ecosystems using credentials harvested from developers who publish to multiple registries.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in pnpm-workspace.yaml Alter Package Scope Definitions and Workspace Protocol Resolution
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in pnpm workspace configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter package scope patterns and workspace protocol dependency resolution, causing pnpm to resolve workspace dependencies from different locations or external registries from what monorepo developers intend during workspace configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Supply Chain Attacks 2026 Escalation Pattern Shows Each Campaign More Sophisticated Than Previous Using Refined Techniques from Prior Waves
description: SafeDep documented that 2026 supply chain attack campaigns followed a consistent escalation pattern where each campaign was more sophisticated than the previous one, with attackers applying lessons learned from defensive responses to refine techniques including improved obfuscation, broader namespace targeting, cross-ecosystem propagation, and provenance attestation bypass.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Astro astro.config.mjs Alter Integration Configuration and Middleware Pipeline Order
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Astro web framework configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter integration loading order and middleware pipeline execution sequence, causing Astro builds to apply different integrations and security middleware from what developers intend when configuring the static site generator.
label: AV-300

title: Code Cooldowns Time-Based Delays Before Incorporating Updated Packages Urged as Primary Defense Against 2026 Supply Chain Wave
description: Experts responding to the May 2026 supply chain attack wave urged security and development teams to introduce time-based delays or code cooldowns before incorporating updated packages from public repositories, to give defenders a chance to take down corrupted code before it is consumed by applications across the internet through automated dependency updates.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Gatsby gatsby-config.ts Alter Plugin Configuration and GraphQL Data Source Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Gatsby static site generator configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter Gatsby plugin configurations and GraphQL data source definitions, causing Gatsby builds to fetch data from different sources or apply different plugin transformations from what developers intend during configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: CISA Issues Advisory for Both September 2025 Shai-Hulud Wave and TJ-Actions Compromise Documenting OIDC Token Extraction Technique Reused in TanStack Attack
description: CISA issued advisories for both the original September 2025 Shai-Hulud wave and the tj-actions/changed-files compromise that first documented the OIDC token-extraction technique subsequently reused in the TanStack Wave 4 attack, establishing a documented technical lineage connecting multiple supply chain attack campaigns through shared exploitation techniques.
label: AV-410

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Docusaurus docusaurus.config.ts Alter Plugin Loading and Navbar Security Link Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Docusaurus documentation platform configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter plugin loading configurations and navigation link definitions, causing documentation sites to load different plugins and display different navigation links from what documentation maintainers review during site configuration.
label: AV-300

title: Malicious Python Packages Exploit Python Import System .pth Files for Interpreter-Level Persistence Requiring No Explicit Import
description: Security researchers documented that malicious Python packages increasingly exploit Python interpreter .pth file persistence mechanisms that execute automatically when the Python interpreter starts without requiring any explicit import statement, making the persistence mechanism significantly harder to detect and remove than traditional postinstall script attacks.
label: AV-400

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Remix remix.config.js Alter Server Routes and Browser Build Configuration
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Remix web framework configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter server route patterns and browser build entry points, causing Remix applications to expose different server routes and build different client-side bundles from what developers intend during full-stack web application configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Lockfile Check Verify Integrity of Lockfiles After Every Package Update to Detect Supply Chain Tampering Before Deployment
description: Security researchers responding to the 2026 supply chain attack wave recommended implementing lockfile integrity checking after every package update operation, verifying that lockfile changes match expected patterns for declared dependency updates and flagging unexpected lockfile modifications that could indicate supply chain tampering or malicious package injection.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Drizzle ORM drizzle.config.ts Alter Database Connection and Migration Source Paths
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Drizzle ORM configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter database connection string references and migration source directory paths, causing Drizzle to connect to different databases or apply migrations from different source directories from what developers specify during ORM configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: npm and PyPI Total Registered Packages Exceed 10 Million Creating Impossible Manual Review Problem for Registry Security Teams
description: Security statistics from 2026 documented that combined npm and PyPI registered packages exceeded 10 million total packages, creating an impossible manual review problem for registry security teams that must rely on automated scanning to identify malicious packages, with attackers exploiting scanner limitations through obfuscation and delayed activation techniques.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in tRPC trpc.config.ts Alter Router Middleware and Authentication Procedure Definitions
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in tRPC TypeScript RPC framework configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter middleware application order and authentication procedure definitions, causing tRPC routers to apply different authentication logic from what API developers review during type-safe API configuration and security review.
label: AV-300

title: Checkmarx KICS Scanner Twice Targeted by TeamPCP Demonstrating Supply Chain Attack Preference for Security Tooling as High-Trust Vector
description: TeamPCP targeted the Checkmarx KICS security scanner twice in separate attacks, demonstrating attackers' strategic preference for compromising security tooling as supply chain attack vectors due to the elevated trust security tools receive in CI/CD pipelines and the irony that security tools run with access to the secrets they are supposed to protect.
label: AV-200

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Zod Schema Definitions Alter Validation Rules and Type Coercion Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Zod TypeScript schema validation library definitions where hidden Unicode characters alter validation rule specifications and type coercion behavior, causing runtime validation to accept or reject different data patterns from what developers intend when defining input validation schemas for API endpoints.
label: AV-300

title: Supply Chain Security Insurance Claims Triple in 2025 as Cyber Insurers Respond to Rising Frequency and Severity of Ecosystem Attacks
description: Stingrai Research documented that supply chain security insurance claims tripled in 2025 as cyber insurers responded to the rising frequency and severity of npm, PyPI, and Docker Hub ecosystem attacks, with insurers increasingly requiring organizations to demonstrate dependency monitoring, lockfile enforcement, and registry security controls as coverage conditions.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Zustand and Jotai State Management Store Definitions Alter Initial State and Action Handler Logic
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Zustand and Jotai React state management library store definitions where hidden Unicode characters alter initial state values and action handler implementations, causing frontend applications to initialize with different state values and handle user actions differently from what developers intend during store definition review.
label: AV-300

title: Package Signing Without Verification Enforcement Creates False Security Theater as Demonstrated by Multiple 2025-2026 Supply Chain Compromises
description: Security researchers documented that package signing implementation without mandatory verification enforcement at installation time creates false security theater, as demonstrated by multiple 2025-2026 supply chain compromises where attackers published unsigned malicious packages alongside signed legitimate ones and consumers installed both without signature verification failures.
label: AV-509

title: Trojan Source Unicode Characters in Convex convex.config.ts Alter Backend Function Definitions and Authentication Configuration
description: Security researchers demonstrated Trojan Source attacks in Convex backend-as-a-service configuration files where hidden Unicode characters alter backend function routing definitions and authentication configuration, causing Convex deployments to expose different function endpoints and apply different authentication requirements from what developers intend during serverless backend configuration review.
label: AV-300

title: Mini Shai-Hulud Open Source Release Democratizes Supply Chain Worm Deployment Enabling Script Kiddies to Launch Ecosystem-Scale Attacks
description: Security researchers warned that the May 13 2026 open source release of the Mini Shai-Hulud malware democratized supply chain worm deployment, enabling attackers with minimal technical sophistication to launch ecosystem-scale attacks using the proven toolchain, significantly lowering the barrier to entry for npm and PyPI supply chain attacks.
label: AV-410
